seoTitle: Make a Confluence Page Full Width (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to make a Confluence page full width in 2026 using the canvas toggle, so wide tables, Gantt charts, and databases display without cramped scrolling.
intro: Cramming wide tables and data-heavy macros into a narrow center column ruins readability. This guide shows how to switch a Confluence page to full-width layout in 2026, giving your content more room to breathe.

steps.heading: Enter edit mode
steps.body: Open your target page and click the pencil icon to enter edit mode.

steps.heading: Find the width toggle
steps.body: Move your cursor to the top center control bar to locate the canvas width options.

steps.heading: Switch to full width
steps.body: Click the outer parallel arrows icon to instantly expand your text area into full-width layout.

steps.heading: Fine-tune with sections
steps.body: For more control, insert a section break block and adjust individual column width sliders.

steps.heading: Publish the change
steps.body: Click Publish to save your visual viewport settings live.

faq.question: Will full-width layout look right on ultra-wide monitors?
faq.answer: Yes, the canvas engine uses relative responsive margins to keep wide layouts uniform across ultra-wide and standard displays.

faq.question: What content benefits most from a full-width layout?
faq.answer: Data-heavy elements like Confluence Databases, Gantt charts, and horizontal tab blocks tend to display much better at full width.

faq.question: Can I preview how it looks before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, it's worth checking your layout across multiple viewport previews before publishing changes live.

faq.question: Can I mix full-width and narrow sections on the same page?
faq.answer: Yes, inserting section breaks lets you adjust column widths independently within a single page.
